prepR4pcm 0.3.0

Reconcile species names between a dataset and a phylogenetic tree

Source: R/reconcile_tree.R
reconcile_tree.Rd

Compares species names in a data frame against tip labels of a phylogenetic tree. This is the core function for preparing data for phylogenetic comparative methods (PGLS, phylogenetic mixed models, etc.).

Usage

reconcile_tree(
  x,
  tree,
  x_species = NULL,
  authority = "col",
  rank = c("species", "subspecies"),
  overrides = NULL,
  db_version = NULL,
  fuzzy = FALSE,
  fuzzy_threshold = 0.9,
  resolve = c("flag", "first"),
  quiet = FALSE
)

Arguments

x

A data frame.

tree

An ape::phylo object, or a character(1) file path to a Newick or Nexus tree file.

x_species

Character(1). Column name in x containing species names. Auto-detected if NULL.

authority

Character(1). Taxonomic authority for synonym resolution: "col" (default), "itis", "gbif", "ncbi", etc. Set to NULL to skip synonym lookup entirely.

rank

Character(1). "species" (default) strips infraspecific epithets during normalisation. "subspecies" retains trinomials.

overrides

A data frame with at least columns name_x and name_y containing pre-built name corrections, or a file path to a CSV. Optional column user_note for documentation. Set to NULL (default) for no overrides.

db_version

Character(1). taxadb database version. NULL uses the latest available.

fuzzy

Logical. Enable fuzzy matching for likely typos? Default FALSE. When TRUE, names that remain unmatched after synonym resolution are compared using component-based string similarity.

fuzzy_threshold

Numeric (0–1). Minimum similarity score for fuzzy matches. Default 0.9 (conservative; catches obvious typos).

resolve

Character(1). How to handle low-confidence matches: "flag" (default) marks fuzzy matches below 0.95 and indirect synonym matches as match_type = "flagged" for manual review. "first" accepts all matches at face value.

quiet

Logical. Suppress progress messages? Default FALSE.

Value

A reconciliation object.

References

Paradis, E. & Schliep, K. (2019) ape 5.0: an environment for modern phylogenetics and evolutionary analyses in R. Bioinformatics 35:526–528. doi:10.1093/bioinformatics/bty633

Examples

data(avonet_subset)
data(tree_jetz)
result <- reconcile_tree(avonet_subset, tree_jetz,
                         x_species = "Species1", authority = NULL)
#> ℹ Reconciling 919 data names vs 657 tree tips
#> ℹ Matching 919 x 657 names through 2 stages...
#> ℹ Stage 1/4: Exact matching...
#> ℹ Stage 2/4: Normalised matching (0 matched so far)...
#> ℹ Stage 3/4: Synonym resolution (657 matched so far)...
#> ℹ Stage 4/4: Fuzzy matching (657 matched so far)...
#> ✔ Matched 657/919 data names to tree tips
print(result)
#> 
#> ── Reconciliation: data vs tree ────────────────────────────────────────────────
#>   Source x: avonet_subset
#>   Source y: phylo (657 tips)
#>   Authority: none
#>   Timestamp: 2026-04-07 09:36:46
#> ℹ Match coverage: [█████████████████████░░░░░░░░░] 71% (657/919)
#> 
#> ── Match summary ──
#> 
#> • Exact: 0 ( 0.0%)
#> • Normalized: 657 (71.5%)
#> • Synonym: 0 ( 0.0%)
#> • Fuzzy: 0 ( 0.0%)
#> • Manual: 0 ( 0.0%)
#> ! Unresolved (x only):262 (28.5%)
#> ! Unresolved (y only):0
#> ! Flagged for review: 0
#> ℹ Use `reconcile_summary()` for details, `reconcile_mapping()` for the full table.
